sanskrit:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः।
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते॥
transliteration:
yoga-yukto viśuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ |
sarva-bhūtātmabhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate ||
literal_meaning: One who is yoked to yoga, whose inner self is purified, whose self is conquered, whose senses are conquered—whose self has become the self of all beings—is not stained even while acting.
contextual_meaning: A person who acts from a state of integrated awareness (yoga) remains inwardly clean, self-regulated, and empathically connected to all life; therefore no action leaves karmic residue.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous attachment: mind and senses are subordinated to a stable, compassionate self-identity that experiences others as extensions of its own being, eliminating defensive reactivity.
trigger_scenario: You’re the team-lead on a tight-deadline project. A colleague lashes out under stress. Instead of retaliating or absorbing blame, you stay calm, help re-frame the task, and later neither ruminate on the incident nor boast about your restraint—your nervous system returns to baseline as if nothing ‘sticky’ happened.
anchor_text: Act from stillness; no stain can stick to a silent heart.
